---
title: Button
description: Action triggers with shadcn-style variants and sizes.
---

A `block_button()` renders a styled `<button type="button">` with six
variants (`default`, `secondary`, `outline`, `ghost`, `destructive`,
`link`) and four sizes (`default`, `sm`, `lg`, `icon`). Optional icons
sit before or after the label.

```{shinylive-r}
#| standalone: true
#| components: [viewer]
#| viewerHeight: 480
{{< include _examples/button.R >}}
```

```r
{{< include _examples/button.R >}}
```

## Relevant functions

- [`block_button(label, variant, size, icon, icon_position, ..., class)`](../../reference/block_button.html)
- [`block_icon(name, class, ...)`](../../reference/block_icon.html)

## Details

`block_button()` always emits `type="button"` so it never accidentally
submits a parent `<form>`. Variants map to single CSS classes
(`sb-button-default`, `sb-button-secondary`, …), so theme overrides
target one selector each. The `icon` argument accepts either a name
from the vendored Lucide sprite (see `block_icon()`) or a pre-built
`htmltools` tag, and `icon_position` chooses whether it sits before or
after the label.

The `size = "icon"` variant produces a square button sized for a
single glyph — pair it with `icon = ...` and an empty `label` for
toolbar-style controls.

## See also

- [Badge](badge.html) — non-interactive labels with the same variant
  vocabulary.
- [Alert](alert.html) — uses the same icon helper for its leading
  glyph.
- [Icon](icon.html) — the vendored Lucide sprite that powers the
  `icon =` argument.
